content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"evaluate\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"autotest/framework/evaluator.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 16, \"end_line\": 61}, \"body\": \"export function evaluate(\\n  tc: TestCase,\\n  actualAgent: string,\\n  exitCode: number\\n): EvalResult {\\n  const expectedAgent: string = tc.expected_agent ?? \\\"\\\";\\n  const expectedAlternatives: string[] = tc.expected_alternatives ?? [];\\n\\n  if (exitCode !== 0) {\\n    return exitCode === 124 ? \\\"TIMEOUT\\\" : \\\"ERROR\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  if (expectedAgent) {\\n    if (expectedAgent === \\\"NO_TASK_CALL\\\") {\\n      // Test expects direct handling (no delegation)\\n      if (!actualAgent || actualAgent === \\\"NO_TASK_CALL\\\") {\\n        return \\\"PASS\\\";\\n      }\\n      // Model delegated when it shouldn't have\\n      return \\\"FAIL_OVER_DELEGATED\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    if (actualAgent === expectedAgent) {\\n      return \\\"PASS\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    if (expectedAlternatives.includes(actualAgent)) {\\n      return \\\"PASS_ALT\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    if (actualAgent === \\\"TASK_USED\\\") {\\n      // Task tool was used but subagent_type couldn't be extracted from transcript.\\n      // The delegation DID happen, we just can't verify which specific agent.\\n      return \\\"PASS_DELEGATED\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    if (!actualAgent || actualAgent === \\\"NO_TASK_CALL\\\") {\\n      return \\\"NO_DELEGATION\\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    return \\\"FAIL\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  // No expected agent specified — exit code 0 is a pass\\n  return exitCode === 0 ? \\\"PASS\\\" : \\\"FAIL\\\";\\n}\"}]"}

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"parseTranscriptFile\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"autotest/framework/parsers/transcript-parser.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 21, \"end_line\": 38}, \"body\": \"export function parseTranscriptFile(path: string): TranscriptEntry[] {\\n  const entries: TranscriptEntry[] = [];\\n  const content = readFileSync(path, \\\"utf-8\\\");\\n  for (const [i, line] of content.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\").entries()) {\\n    const trimmed = line.trim();\\n    if (!trimmed) continue;\\n    try {\\n      entries.push(JSON.parse(trimmed) as TranscriptEntry);\\n    } catch {\\n      entries.push({\\n        type: \\\"parse_error\\\",\\n        line: i + 1,\\n        raw: trimmed.slice(0, 200),\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return entries;\\n}\"}]"}

[]) {\\n    // Derive model_slug from model string (same tr '/@:' '_' logic as runner-base.sh)\\n    const modelSlug = run.model.replace(/[\\\\/@:]/g, \\\"_\\\");\\n\\n    const entry: TestEntry = {\\n      test_id: run.test_id,\\n      model_slug: modelSlug,\\n      model: run.model,\\n      duration_seconds: run.duration_seconds,\\n      exit_code: run.exit_code,\\n      // Fields now available from enriched RunEntry:\\n      total_tokens: run.total_tokens ?? 0,\\n      prompt_tokens: run.prompt_tokens ?? 0,\\n      completion_tokens: run.completion_tokens ?? 0,\\n      total_turns: run.turns ?? 0,\\n      total_retries: run.retries ?? 0,\\n      time_to_first_tool_ms: run.time_to_first_tool_ms ?? null,\\n      api_time_ms: undefined, // Not in RunEntry; wall_time_ms approximates\\n      wall_time_ms: run.wall_time_ms ?? (run.duration_seconds * 1000),\\n      cost_usd: run.cost_usd ?? null,\\n      unique_tools: run.unique_tools ?? [],\\n    };\\n\\n    if (!modelResults[modelSlug]) modelResults[modelSlug] = [];\\n    modelResults[modelSlug].push(entry);\\n  }\\n\\n  return [config, summary, modelResults];\\n}\"}]"}

[]) {\\n      if (block.type === \\\"tool_use\\\" && block.name === \\\"Task\\\") {\\n        const agent = block.input?.subagent_type ?? \\\"\\\";\\n        if (agent) return agent;\\n        // Task was used but subagent_type not present\\n        return \\\"TASK_USED\\\";\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return \\\"\\\";\\n}\"}]"}

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"findFilesRecursive\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"autotest/framework/aggregator.ts\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 42, \"end_line\": 61}, \"body\": \"function findFilesRecursive(dir: string, filename: string): string[] {\\n  const results: string[] = [];\\n  if (!existsSync(dir)) return results;\\n\\n  function walk(d: string) {\\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(d)) {\\n      const full = join(d, entry);\\n      try {\\n        const stat = statSync(full);\\n        if (stat.isDirectory()) walk(full);\\n        else if (entry === filename) results.push(full);\\n      } catch {\\n        // Skip inaccessible entries\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  walk(dir);\\n  return results.sort();\\n}\"}]"}

content.thinking:
Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis of the `autotest/framework/` directory architecture. Let me organize what I know:

## Key Modules

1. **`types.ts`** - Central type definitions. Contains interfaces for all data structures used across the framework: `TestCase`, `TranscriptEntry`, `Metrics`, `DebugTurn`, `TestResult`, `RunEntry`, `ResultsSummary`, `AggregateStats`, etc.

2. **`runner-base.sh`** - Shell orchestration layer. Entry point for running test suites. Calls `execute-test.sh` for each test case.


3. **`execute-test.sh`** - Executes individual tests via `claudish`, capturing the transcript and debug output to files, then generating metadata and metrics.
4. **`parsers/transcript-parser.ts`** - Handles parsing JSONL transcript files and extracting key information like agent types and replay turns from the transcript entries.
5. **`parsers/debug-log-parser.ts`** - Parses the debug logs to extract metrics including turn counts, token usage, costs, and timing data using regex patterns.
6. **`evaluator.ts`** - Takes test results and compares the actual agent against expectations to determine pass/fail status and handle edge cases like timeouts.
7. **`aggregator.ts`** - Reads metadata from completed runs and uses the parsers and evaluator to compile a comprehensive results summary across all tests.
8. **`compar...`** - Still reading through the remaining components... Continuing the data flow through the remaining tools - the transcript parser extracts the agent response, the debug log parser pulls metrics like token counts and timing, then the evaluator compares the actual output against expected results and writes the summary. The comparator tool loads those summaries from different run directories to build side-by-side performance tables, while the replay tool reconstructs the conversation turns from transcripts and debug logs for interactive review.

Looking at the connectivity, types.ts is the foundation that everything depends on, then the two parsers and evaluator build on that, aggregator orchestrates them all as a CLI tool, comparator reads aggregator's output for comparisons, and replay uses the parsers independently. The dependency chain flows from types up through the parsers and evaluator, then into the three standalone CLI tools.

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The framework uses a **two-phase architecture**: Shell orchestrates execution (runner → execute), then TypeScript handles analysis (aggregator → comparator). This separation keeps runtime concerns (claudish, timeouts, file capture) in bash and data analysis in typed TypeScript.

`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Here is the full architectural analysis:

---

## 1. Key Modules and Responsibilities

| Module | Language | Responsibility |
|--------|----------|----------------|
| `types.ts` | TypeScript | Shared type definitions: `TestCase`, `TranscriptEntry`, `Metrics`, `RunEntry`, `ResultsSummary`, `EvalResult`, etc. — the schema layer for the entire framework |
| `runner-base.sh` | Bash | Suite orchestrator: iterates test cases, manages parallelism, invokes `execute-test.sh` per test |
| `execute-test.sh` | Bash | Single-test executor: calls `claudish`, captures `transcript.jsonl` + `debug.log`, writes `meta.json` |
| `parsers/transcript-parser.ts` | TypeScript | Parses JSONL transcript files into `TranscriptEntry[]`; also extracts `subagent_type` from Task tool calls (`extractAgentFromTranscript`) and builds replay views (`buildReplayTurns`) |
| `parsers/debug-log-parser.ts` | TypeScript | Parses claudish debug logs with 17 regex constants; extracts token counts, cost, timing, tool call sequences → `Metrics` |
| `evaluator.ts` | TypeScript | Pure evaluation logic: `evaluate(tc, actualAgent, exitCode)` → `EvalResult` (PASS / PASS_ALT / PASS_DELEGATED / NO_DELEGATION / FAIL / TIMEOUT / ERROR) |
| `aggregator.ts` | TypeScript | Post-run CLI tool: walks result directories, calls both parsers + evaluator, writes `results-summary.json` with per-run stats |
| `comparator.ts` | TypeScript | Cross-run CLI tool: loads `results-summary.json` from two directories, builds side-by-side model comparison tables |
| `replay.ts` | TypeScript | Interactive replay CLI: reads transcript + metrics for a single test, formats turn-by-turn output for review |

---

## 2. Data Flow

```
runner-base.sh
  └─► execute-test.sh  (once per test × model)
        └─► claudish --model X --debug
              ├─► transcript.jsonl   (JSONL: every assistant/user/tool message)
              ├─► debug.log          (claudish internals: tokens, timing, cost)
              └─► meta.json          (test metadata: test_id, model, exit_code, duration)

─── Post-run analysis (TypeScript CLI tools) ───

aggregator.ts  <results-dir>
  ├─► findFilesRecursive()  ──► meta.json files (one per run)
  ├─► transcript-parser.ts  ──► parseTranscriptFile()  → TranscriptEntry[]
  │                         └─► extractAgentFromTranscript()  → actualAgent string
  ├─► debug-log-parser.ts   ──► parseDebugLog()  → Metrics (tokens, cost, turns, timing)
  ├─► evaluator.ts          ──► evaluate(tc, actualAgent, exitCode)  → EvalResult
  └─► writes results-summary.json  (central output artifact)

comparator.ts  <dir-A> <dir-B>
  └─► loadRunData()  ──► results-summary.json  (from each dir)
        └─► buildComparison()  → side-by-side table (pass rates, token cost, latency)

replay.ts  <test-dir>
  ├─► transcript-parser.ts  ──► parseTranscriptFile() + buildReplayTurns()
  └─► debug-log-parser.ts   ──► parseDebugLog()  → Metrics
        └─► interactiveReplay()  → formatted turn-by-turn output
```

---

## 3. Most Central Module

**`types.ts`** has the highest connectivity — it is imported by every TypeScript module. It is the schema foundation that makes all modules interoperable.

**`aggregator.ts`** has the highest *functional* centrality — it is the only module that calls all three analysis components (both parsers + evaluator), consumes raw execution output, and produces the `results-summary.json` that downstream tools (`comparator`, suite-specific analyzers) depend on. It is the hub of the post-run analysis phase.

---

## 4. Dependency Hierarchy

```
Layer 0 (no deps):
  types.ts

Layer 1 (depend only on types):
  parsers/transcript-parser.ts  →  types.ts
  parsers/debug-log-parser.ts   →  types.ts
  evaluator.ts                  →  types.ts

Layer 2 (depend on Layer 1):
  aggregator.ts   →  transcript-parser, debug-log-parser, evaluator, types
  replay.ts       →  transcript-parser, debug-log-parser, types

Layer 3 (depends on aggregator output):
  comparator.ts   →  types, results-summary.json (runtime artifact from aggregator)

Shell layer (executes, does not import TS):
  runner-base.sh  →  execute-test.sh
  execute-test.sh →  claudish CLI
```

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **`evaluator.ts` is intentionally a pure function** — it has no I/O, no file reads, no side effects. This makes it trivially testable in isolation. The aggregator owns orchestration; the evaluator owns only the verdict logic.
- The **`debug-log-parser.ts`** has 17 regex constants (`RE_*`) for parsing claudish's internal log format. This tight coupling to claudish's log schema means any change to claudish's output format would require updating these regexes — a hidden fragility point.
- `comparator.ts` is the only module that doesn't touch raw execution files — it only reads `results-summary.json`. This makes it a **read-only analytical layer** fully decoupled from execution, which is good separation of concerns.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`
